summary: Across 8 BULLISH signals, the scanner achieved an 87.5% win rate on 5-day returns (avg +10.98%) with only one loser (RDNT at -1.32%). Volume ratio is the strongest feature separator: signals with vol_ratio >= 2.0 went 6/6 (100% WR, avg +14.35%) while vol_ratio < 2.0 went 1/2 (50% WR, avg +0.88%). GAP_AND_GO pattern dominated at 100% WR (5/5, avg +15.93%) vs RANGE_BREAKOUT at 50% WR (1/2, avg +0.88%).
top_3_improvements: 1. Add conditional rule: reject RANGE_BREAKOUT when volume_ratio < 2.0 — this single rule eliminates the only 5d loser (RDNT: vol_ratio=1.49, ret_5d=-1.32%) while preserving all winners

2. Lower min_rs_vs_spy from 20.0 to 15.0 to ~3x signal count — all 8 signals clustered in a narrow 22.2-28.6 band indicating the 20.0 floor is aggressively filtering; lowering to 15 adds candidates without degrading the demonstrated edge since even the lowest RS signals (ALGM at 22.2, ENS at 22.5) were winners

3. Favor vol_ratio >= 2.3 signals with higher position sizing — the 4 signals at vol_ratio >= 2.3 (MRNA, RIVN, FORM, LFUS) averaged +17.27% on 5d at 100% WR vs +4.69% avg at 75% WR for the 4 signals below 2.3

feature_findings.finding: Best separator. vol_ratio >= 2.3: 4/4 winners (100% WR, avg 5d +17.27%). vol_ratio 2.0-2.29: 2/2 winners (100% WR, avg 5d +8.50%). vol_ratio < 2.0: 1/2 winners (50% WR, avg 5d +0.88%). The sole loser RDNT had vol_ratio=1.49. Threshold of 2.0 perfectly separates 100% WR group from mixed group.

GAP_AND_GO: 5/5 = 100% WR on 5d, avg +15.93%. VCP_BREAKOUT: 1/1 = 100% WR, avg +6.43%. RANGE_BREAKOUT: 1/2 = 50% WR, avg +0.88%. GAP_AND_GO produced the 4 largest 5d returns (MRNA +25.78%, RIVN +21.88%, FORM +16.01%, ALGM +10.56%).

macd_exp=Y: 4/4 = 100% WR on 5d, avg +11.21% (MRNA +25.78, ALGM +10.56, LFUS +5.41, CLF +3.07). macd_exp=N: 3/4 = 75% WR, avg +10.75%. The only loser (RDNT) had macd_exp=N. Positive but modest separation due to small sample.

High ADX (>31): 4/5 = 80% WR, avg 5d +11.29% (includes loser RDNT at ADX=32.4). Low ADX (<=31): 3/3 = 100% WR, avg 5d +10.46%. ADX does not clearly separate winners from losers; the loser RDNT sat at 32.4 which is mid-range. No evidence to cap or raise ADX threshold.

High RS (>=25): 3/4 = 75% WR, avg +9.91% (includes loser RDNT at 25.4). Low RS (<25): 4/4 = 100% WR, avg +12.05%. Counter-intuitively lower RS within the passing range performed better. All signals passed comfortably above the 20.0 minimum (range 22.2-28.6), suggesting the floor is functional but not discriminating winners.
All 8 signals had near_52w=N. No variance to analyze. This feature provides zero discrimination in the current dataset.

pat_q >= 65: 4/4 = 100% WR, avg 5d +13.72%. pat_q <= 55: 3/4 = 75% WR, avg 5d +8.24%. Higher pattern quality correlates with better WR, though the biggest single winner (MRNA +25.78%) had pat_q=55. The loser RDNT had pat_q=50 (minimum allowed).

filter_rules: Reject RANGE_BREAKOUT when volume_ratio < 2.0 (RDNT: vol_ratio=1.49, ret_5d=-1.32% was the only loser; CLF: vol_ratio=1.51, ret_5d=+3.07% is marginal and also filtered but acceptable cost)
Reject any signal when volume_ratio < 1.5 AND macd_exp=N (targets exact RDNT profile: vol_ratio=1.49, macd_exp=N, only loser)
Boost confidence tier for GAP_AND_GO when volume_ratio >= 2.3 (4/4 = 100% WR, avg 5d +17.27%, these are the highest-conviction signals)
Flag RANGE_BREAKOUT signals for manual review — 50% WR (1/2) with avg 5d +0.88% is significantly below GAP_AND_GO's 100% WR and +15.93% avg
signal_count_diagnosis: At 0.28 signals/day, the scanner is roughly 3.5x below the 1/day target. The most likely bottleneck is min_rs_vs_spy=20.0 — requiring a stock to outperform SPY by 20%+ is extremely restrictive and explains why all 8 passing signals cluster in a narrow 22.2-28.6 RS band. Secondary bottleneck is min_composite_score=30, which aggregates multiple filters and may reject signals that pass individual thresholds. The tight clustering of all features (ADX 27.9-34.8, vol_ratio 1.49-2.80, RSI 64.1-71.7) suggests several filters are binding simultaneously, but rs_vs_spy is the most restrictive in the general stock population since few stocks sustain 20%+ relative strength.
signal_count_fix: Lower min_rs_vs_spy from 20.0 to 15.0 and reduce min_composite_score from 30 to 25. The RS filter is the primary bottleneck — in the general market, far more stocks cluster in the 12-20% RS range than above 20%. Lowering to 15.0 should approximately 3x signal count by admitting the large pool of moderately strong stocks. This should not significantly hurt win rate: within the current data, the lowest RS signals (ALGM 22.2, ENS 22.5, MRNA 22.6) were all winners with avg 5d return of +14.26%, suggesting that RS values well below the current cluster are likely still profitable. Simultaneously lowering min_composite_score from 30 to 25 provides incremental signal expansion.
prefilter_scoring_guide: Score candidates 0-100 using: volume_ratio (40 pts: 0 if <1.5, 15 if 1.5-2.0, 30 if 2.0-2.3, 40 if >=2.3), pattern type (30 pts: 30 for GAP_AND_GO, 20 for VCP_BREAKOUT, 10 for RANGE_BREAKOUT), macd_exp (15 pts: 15 if Y, 0 if N), pattern_quality (15 pts: 15 if >=65, 8 if 50-64, 0 if <50). Signals scoring >=70 are high-conviction (in backtest, MRNA/FORM/ALGM/LFUS would score 70-85 and averaged +14.44% on 5d). Scores below 40 should be rejected — RDNT, the only loser, would score approximately 25 (vol 0 + pattern 10 + macd 0 + quality 8).
win_probability_model: Base 5d win rate: 87.5% (7/8). Adjust using three dominant features: (1) volume_ratio >= 2.0 → 100% WR (6/6) vs <2.0 → 50% (1/2); weight this +/- 20%. (2) Pattern: GAP_AND_GO → 100% (5/5), VCP_BREAKOUT → 100% (1/1), RANGE_BREAKOUT → 50% (1/2); weight +/- 15%. (3) macd_exp=Y → 100% (4/4) vs N → 75% (3/4); weight +/- 10%. Formula: start at 60% base. Add +20% if vol_ratio >= 2.0, +10% if vol_ratio >= 2.3. Add +15% if GAP_AND_GO, +10% if VCP_BREAKOUT, +0% if RANGE_BREAKOUT. Add +10% if macd_exp=Y. Cap at 95%. Example: a GAP_AND_GO with vol_ratio=2.5 and macd_exp=Y scores 60+20+10+15+10 = 95% win probability. A RANGE_BREAKOUT with vol_ratio=1.5 and macd_exp=N scores 60+0+0+0 = 60% — marginal, consider skipping.
